description_long: This is a robust, single-bladed axe with a long, cylindrical wooden handle that appears well-worn and stained from use. The handle is straight, made of dark brown wood, and shows subtle grain patterns and some surface wear. The axe head is metallic, featuring a broad, slightly curved blade on one side and a pointed spike on the opposite end, suggesting a tactical or specialized function. The blade is matte black or dark gray, with a smooth but utilitarian finish, and the poll is squared off, providing a flat striking surface. The mounting of the head to the handle is traditional, with the metal head fitted securely onto the wood, possibly wedged or pinned. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on the handle or the blade.
description: A sturdy, single-bladed axe with a straight wooden handle and a dark metal head featuring both a blade and a spike. The tool has a utilitarian, worn appearance suitable for heavy-duty use.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top-down, showing the cylindrical wooden handle and the narrow profile of the head.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, curved blade with a flat poll and a spike on the opposite end.
description_view_2: The bottom-up view highlights the pointed spike at the end of the axe head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the blade's curvature and the squared poll, with the handle's wood grain visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, dark brown, with visible grain and a smooth, slightly worn surface; no special grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad, slightly curved blade with a squared poll and a pointed spike on the opposite end; utilitarian and functional with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or pinned mounting, with the metal head fitted securely onto the wooden handle.
